name: How to create and setup Microsoft clarity on a website
description: Learn how to set up Microsoft Clarity on your website and analyze user behavior. Our step-by-step guide ensures compliance with Google's character limit requirements, so you can improve site performance and user experience.
main_title: How to create and setup Microsoft clarity on a website
richtext:
What is Microsoft Clarity?

Microsoft Clarity is a free application that assists marketers and website owners in evaluating user behavioral on webpages. It provides information and insights, like heatmaps and session records which helps website administrators to improve user experience. Microsoft Clarity is a powerful and user-friendly application for website owners that can be easily installed on any website.

‍

Step-by-Step Guide on How to Setup Microsoft Clarity?

  1. Go to the Microsoft Clarity website (https://clarity.microsoft.com).
  1. Sign in to your Microsoft account. If you don't have one, you can create a new account.
  1. Click on the "Add website" button.
  1. Enter your website's Name and URL in the designated field and click "Add new project".
  1. Copy the tracking code provided by Microsoft Clarity and paste it into the code of your website. The code should be placed immediately before the closing </head> tag.
  1. Wait for a few minutes to allow Microsoft Clarity to start collecting data.
  2. Go back to the Microsoft Clarity dashboard and you should see your website's data being displayed.
  1. You can now use Microsoft Clarity to analyze your website's user experience, including session recordings, heatmaps, funnels, and more.
  2. Regularly check your Microsoft Clarity dashboard for any insights and make changes to your website accordingly to improve user experience.

‍

Note: To use Microsoft Clarity, you need to have administrative access to your website's code, and the ability to add code snippets to it.
